item.title: Show HN: Karyfy – AI job-search workspace (matching, tracking, interview prep)
item.description:
Hi HN. I'm the solo developer of Karyfy. I built it because my own job search had turned into juggling six tabs: one board to find roles, a separate tool to tailor each resume, a spreadsheet to track where every application stood, docs for interview notes, and constant searches for how to answer specific questions. The overhead of applying kept getting in the way of actually applying. Karyfy puts that in one place: - Matching that ranks live job postings against your actual resume, instead of keyword filtering - Application pipeline tracking - An interview prep workspace that builds a study pack from the specific job you're applying to - Career planning with courses, and salary data for negotiation What it does not do: it does not mass-apply or spray generic applications for you. I think auto-apply bots make the noise problem worse, so the goal is fewer, better-matched roles and help doing the actual work well. Honest state of things: it's early and built by one person. Matching quality is the part I most want feedback on. The free tier is usable without paying: daily matches, resume tailoring, a full interview package, and salary and company previews, so you can test the core product without a credit card.

Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48963309

Points: 1

# Comments: 0

item.title: Show HN: Ilya Sutskever's AI reading list into a learning RPG – using kimi k3
item.description:
I wanted to take kimi k3 for a spin. It turned my simple one sentence prompt to this. Repo here. https://github.com/praveer13/ilya-papers-quest

Well, I'm mindblown. Very humbling for me as a software engineer. Took couple hours for it to build this completely autonomously. And it was all from its mobile app.

It couldn't render this though from within the app - it does have a feature to preview any website and publish it on kimi's domain - but it didn't work for this. I had to put it on github pages.

It doesn't store anything btw - all progress is tracked in your browser storage.

Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48963286

Points: 1

# Comments: 0
